Name: Blaziken
Effects: Search your discard pile for a [R] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Blaziken. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Cresselia
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, remove 2 damage counters from Cresselia.
You may discard 2 Energy attached to Cresselia. If you do, remove all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If the Defending Pokémon is Cresselia, this attack's base damage is 100.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: You may have your opponent switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 or his or her Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage. If the Defending Pokémon is not Asleep, that Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

If the Defending Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage. Discard that Pokémon Tool card, look at your opponent's hand, and discard any Pokémon Tool cards of the same name you find there.

Name: Porygon-Z
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a basic Energy card from your hand. Porygon-Z is the same type as that Energy Card until the end of your turn. This power can't be used if Porygon-Z is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Sceptile
Ability-Effect: Each basic [G] Energy card attached to your [G] Pokémon provides [G][G] Energy instead. You can't use more than 1 Wild Growth Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Swampert
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [W] or [F] Energy attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Swampert is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each basic Energy card attached to Tangrowth to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )
Remove 3 damage counters from Tangrowth. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Togekiss from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 10 cards of your deck, choose as many basic Energy cards as you like, and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage. If tails, remove 3 damage counters from Togekiss.

Name: Altaria
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Altaria by attacks from your opponent's Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Beedrill
Effects: Does 30 damage times the number of Beedrill you have in play.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: After your attack, remove from 1 of your Pokémon the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Claydol
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose up to 2 cards from your hand and put them on the bottom of your deck in any order. If you do, draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. This power can't be used if Claydol is affected by a Special Condition.

Name: Dialga
Effects: Draw a card.
You may return all Energy cards attached to Dialga to your hand. If you do, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from the Defending Pokémon and shuffle that card into your opponent's deck.

Name: Exploud
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If the first coin is heads, this attack does 50 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If the first coin is tails, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. ) If the second coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If the second coin is tails, your opponent can't play any Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

You may discard a [R] or [D] Energy attached to Houndoom. If you discard a [R] Energy, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If you discard a [D] Energy, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Hypno
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, move all damage counters from Hypno to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Kingler
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon would be be Knocked Out by this attack, Kingler does 30 damage to itself.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon Tool Card, a Supporter card, and a basic Energy card, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Latias
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters and all Special Conditions from Latias.
Discard a [R] Energy and a [W] Energy attached to Latias.

Name: Latios
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to Latios. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard 3 Energy attached to Latios.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Choose 1 face-down Prize card (yours or your opponent's) and put it face up. If that card is a Supporter card, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (That card remains face up for the rest of the game. )
During your next turn, if an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage.

Name: Milotic
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Milotic by your opponent's Pokémon LV. X.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Palkia
Effects: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If there is any Stadium card in play, discard it.

You may flip a coin. If heads, discard all Energy attached to Palkia and put the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it on top of your opponent's deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.

Name: Primeape
Ability-Effect: If Primeape has any damage counters on it, Primeape's attacks do 40 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Primeape is now Confused. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Slowking
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: During your next turn, Slowking's Psych Up attack's base damage is 60.

Name: Unown [H]
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown H, Unown E, Unown A, and Unown L on your Bench, you may remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. (If you can't discard a card from your hand, this attack does nothing. )

Name: Wailord
Ability-Effect: As long as Wailord remains Asleep between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Wailord.
Effects: If Wailord has any damage counters on it, Wailord is now Asleep.

Name: Weezing
Ability-Effect: At any time between turns, each player puts 1 more damage counter on his or her Poisoned Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

Name: Wigglytuff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is now Asleep. This power can't be used if Wigglytuff is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If Igglybuff is anywhere under Wigglytuff, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Arbok
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 10 damage plus 40 more damage. If the Defending Pokémon is not Poisoned, that Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Cacturne
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Combusken
Effects: During your next turn, each of Combusken's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Floatzel

Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Gorebyss
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.
If Gorebyss has any [P] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Granbull
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.

Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [G] Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Hariyama
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.
If Hariyama has fewer remaining HP than the Defending Pokémon, this attack's base damage is 80.

Name: Huntail

If Huntail has any [D] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage and discard a Special Energy card, if any, attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Linoone
Effects: Move a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that already has a Pokémon Tool attached to it). (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing. )
Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Loudred
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Magcargo
Ability-Effect: Magcargo can't be Asleep or Paralyzed.
Effects: You may discard the top card of your deck. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

Name: Marshtomp
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon and 10 damage to each of your opponent's other Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Metapod

If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from Metapod and put it onto Metapod. (This counts as evolving Metapod. ) Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Metapod is affected by a Special Condition.

Name: Porygon2

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Effects: You may flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, this attack's base damage is 30.

Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a [G] Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Illumise is affected by a Special Condition.

Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon. ) You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

Name: Cresselia LV.X
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from either player's Pokémon to another Pokémon (yours or your opponent's). This power can't be used if Cresselia is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, take 1 more Prize card.
Ex-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Cresselia. Cresselia LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous Level.

Name: Darkrai LV.X
Ability-Effect: Each basic [D] Energy card attached to your [D] Pokémon now has the effect "If the Pokémon [D] Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). " You can't use more than 1 Dark Shadow Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, the Defending Pokémon is still Asleep. If both of them are tails, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Ex-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Darkrai. Darkrai LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous Level.

Name: Dialga LV.X
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your turn ends. If both of them are tails, after your opponent draws a card at the beginning of his or her next turn, his or her turn ends. This power can't be used if Dialga is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: During your next turn, Dialga can't use [M] Flash.
Ex-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Dialga. Dialga LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous Level.

Name: Palkia LV.X
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, you switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Palkia is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may move all Energy cards attached to Palkia to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. (Ignore this effect if you don't have any Benched Pokémon. )
Ex-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Palkia. Palkia LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous Level.
